question.text: Within how many days from the date of receipt of money must Indian companies report the receipt of consideration for fresh issuance of shares to foreign investors under FDI?

answer.text: Within 15 days from the date of receipt of money
Within 30 days from the date of receipt of money
Within 60 days from the date of receipt of money
Within 45 days from the date of receipt of money

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Indian companies are required to report the details of the receipt of consideration for issue of shares/convertible debentures/warrants, through an AD Category-I bank, together with FIRC copies and KYC report on the non-resident investor, within 30 days from the date of receipt of the money. **The Trap:** This 30-day reporting for receipt of money is different from the 30-day reporting for issue of shares (Form FC-GPR). Both have 30-day timelines but for different events. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The reporting sequence is: (1) Report receipt of money within 30 days, (2) Issue shares within 180 days, (3) File Form FC-GPR within 30 days of share issuance.
